Roadmap

5 Key Steps

Each step builds on the previous — from understanding the metrics to driving measurable improvement.

01
Foundation
Understand MTBF & MTTR
Completed ✓

All department members must understand MTBF and MTTR — including concepts, calculation formulas, and the specific runtime hours used.

MTBF = Total Uptime ÷ Number of Failures  |  MTTR = Total Repair Time ÷ Number of Repairs

✅ Step 1 Complete. Full team briefing conducted. All members understand MTBF & MTTR concepts, formulas, and the correct runtime hours used in KPI calculations.
02
Alignment
Data Verification with Planning Department
Completed ✓

Coordinated with Planning Department to define and lock the official runtime standard per production area — used as Single Source of Truth for all KPI calculations.

✅ Step 2 Complete — Key Outcome:
• DC & PIM: Runtime revised 20 hrs → 24 hrs/day (updated & aligned with Planning)
• TP, DIJ Single Unit, AS BB: Confirmed at 20 hrs/day (no change)
• Planning Team alignment meeting completed. CMMS PM II standard updated accordingly.
Alignment meeting with Planning Department
MBK Holiday & Planning Schedule — Verified
CMMS PM II — Standard Part List Updated & Aligned
03
Standard Setting
Set Annual Standard Targets
In Progress

Use the verified baseline data to set annual MTBF & MTTR targets per line. Broken down monthly and quarterly for continuous tracking and accountability.

Line / Area Runtime / Day Change Status
DC — Die Cast24 hrs20 → 24 hrs ↑✓ Revised
PIM — Precision Injection Molding24 hrs20 → 24 hrs ↑✓ Revised
TP — Trim Press20 hrsNo change✓ Confirmed
DIJ Single Unit20 hrsNo change✓ Confirmed
AS BB — Assembly Block20 hrsNo change✓ Confirmed
04
Execution
Trial Run & Continuous Monitoring
Upcoming

Deploy targets in live Operations. Monitor continuously via the Top 3 Downtime Dashboard. Alert when MTTR exceeds threshold. Report to Management monthly.

Trial Run — reference view 1
Trial Run — reference view 2
05
Improvement
Improve MTBF & MTTR by 5%
Upcoming

Drive MTBF up by 5% and MTTR down by 5% using three structured improvement tools applied in a continuous loop.

Priority Framework
Eisenhower Matrix
Categorize failure actions by Urgency vs. Importance to focus team resources on highest-impact interventions first.
Root Cause Tool
5 Whys Analysis
Drill to the root cause of each top failure by asking "Why?" five times. Prevents treating symptoms instead of underlying causes.
Data Source
Top 3 Downtime Dashboard
Data-driven analysis of the top 3 failure sources per line. Updated weekly to reflect current performance trends.
Improvement Loop: Top 3 Downtime → Select #1 → 5 Whys → Eisenhower Action Plan → Execute → Measure → Repeat until +5% MTBF / −5% MTTR achieved
